hooks.description: Deny destructive shell commands by default

hooks.prompt: Confirm this MCP call is for a legitimate HR workflow (intake, review, lookup, hiring, performance, offboard), not data exfiltration or bulk export to an unknown destination. If in doubt, do not block but note the concern.

hooks.description: Validate legal language in PIP, feedback, and performance documents
Block writes that appear to contain PII or sensitive HR data
Deny destructive shell commands by default
Log when team metrics are analyzed
Validate JSON output and schema when available

hooks.prompt: If the file just written or edited is an intake (intake-*.json), an eval or report (candidate-report, *report*.md, burnout-risk-assessment, performance-diagnosis-report), or a PIP/feedback doc (*PIP*.md, *pip*.md), suggest the next step in one line: e.g. run /clous:eval, /clous:review-packet, /clous:burnout, /clous:perf-diagnose, or share with recruiter/HR.
Classify intent (hiring, performance, onboarding, org planning, people ops, compliance). If it matches, suggest the most relevant /clous:* commands or agents from the clous-hreng plugin.

hooks.description: Load team config and house rules if present

hooks.prompt: Before compaction, summarize decisions, constraints, open questions, and files touched in concise bullets.

If the response includes legal or termination guidance, add a brief 'not legal advice' note and a jurisdiction caveat. If the response recommended a PIP or termination, remind the user to involve HR/legal before any action.
When a subagent (hreng-hiring, hreng-performance, hreng-org) has just finished, append a one-liner to the session audit: 'Subagent X produced Y' with a brief deliverable summary.
